title: double free of an interop SurfaceTexture handle (reactor frees what detachFromGLContext released)
category: double-free across the Flutter engine (C++) ↔ Android SurfaceTexture (JNI) boundary
crash_site: Flutter Android app (Impeller GLES) crashes after returning to foreground: a GL texture handle is freed twice — once by Android's SurfaceTexture.detachFromGLContext and again by the engine's reactor.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: TextureGLES::~TextureGLES (where the engine-side free is issued) and SurfaceTexture.detachFromGLContext (the JNI release). The defect is the reactor tracking an externally-owned handle as if it owns it, so it later double-frees.
ground_truth.fix_summary: An external SurfaceTexture GL handle was registered with the reactor as a normal owned handle; SurfaceTexture.detachFromGLContext already releases the texture, so the reactor's later collection frees it a second time (after foregrounding), corrupting a handle that may be in active use. Fix: track external handles (CreateHandle gains an external_handle arg) so the reactor does not free them.

admission.note: Authored from PR #170284 (fixes #152459) body+diff. crash != cause: the second free happens in the reactor after foregrounding; cause is CreateHandle registering an externally-owned (SurfaceTexture/JNI) handle as reactor-owned. NOTE: changed files are engine C++/Android JNI only -> exercises the C++↔JNI boundary, NOT the Dart extractor; pure-Dart-language coverage should use a dart-lang/sdk instance. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/170284 (fixes #152459) — don't double free surface texture interop objects; fix 2d30ce56feb7, base ee0eb22cdbea.
